description: Runtime instrumentation for queue and turn lifecycle, phase transitions, provider stream diagnostics, tool-call argument progress, and tool execution telemetry.

userStories.title: Capture queue and turn lifecycle telemetry
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want queue and turn lifecycle records so that blocked queued messages can be traced to the active turn.
acceptanceCriteria: Record telemetry when a user/system message is accepted for a routed session, including piboSessionId and queue depth where available
Record queuedBehind count or equivalent queue position metadata before processing starts
Record turn start and terminal status for ok, error, aborted, and timeout-equivalent outcomes where observable
Record abort, clear, and dispose lifecycle events as telemetry updates where applicable
Add tests or fixtures proving queued messages remain correlated to the active turn
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (409 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Added src/core/runtime-telemetry.ts best-effort recorder for message queued, message started, message finished, session_error, abort/dispose/kill_all, and clear_queue lifecycle events.
Wired PiboSessionRouter to a telemetry store through the new telemetryStore option or PiboDataSessionStore.getTelemetryStore().
Telemetry rows store ids, queue depth, queuedBehind, timestamps, status, phase summaries, and session metadata only; no transcripts or raw provider payloads are captured.

userStories.title: Capture turn phase transitions from runtime events
userStories.description: As an operator, I want explicit phase timelines so that I do not have to infer active state from normalized event names.
acceptanceCriteria: Create telemetry phases for queued, message_started, provider_request or provider_stream, assistant_text, tool_args, tool_execution, finish, abort, error, and timeout when those states occur; capture prompt_build, reasoning, and continuation when visible through existing runtime events
Open phases include startedAt and lastProgressAt; closed phases include endedAt, durationMs, and terminal status
Missing terminal events remain visible as open phases instead of being silently hidden
Phase records link to providerRequestId or toolCallId when known
Tests cover normal assistant response, tool call response, aborted response, and error response event sequences
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Minimum required phase set must be stable for CLI tests; extra visible phases are allowed.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (412 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Added normalized-event phase capture in src/core/runtime-telemetry.ts for assistant_text, reasoning, tool_args, tool_execution, provider_stream, finish, abort, and error phase timelines.
Phase rows are metadata-only, link to the Pibo turn and toolCallId when known, and never store assistant text, reasoning content, raw provider payloads, or full tool arguments.
Open phases remain inspectable until terminal events close them; terminal turn outcomes close all open phases with ok/error/aborted status as appropriate.

userStories.title: Capture provider request lifecycle telemetry
userStories.description: As a provider engineer, I want lifecycle telemetry for each model request so that I can distinguish no response, partial stream, completion, abort, and error cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Record provider, API, model, transport, service tier when known, startedAt, HTTP/status summary, header/status summary, first byte time, completion time, terminal status, and links to normalized events where available
Record lastRawEventAt, lastNormalizedEventAt, upstreamResponseId when observed, bytesReceived when feasible, rawEventCount, normalizedEventCount, parseErrorCount, and unknownEventCount
Metadata telemetry is captured independently of raw Pi event forwarding settings unless telemetry itself is explicitly disabled
Provider telemetry updates are best-effort and do not throw into the streaming path
Tests cover completed, aborted, errored, no-first-byte, and partial/still-open provider request states
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in provider lifecycle telemetry batch on 2026-05-16. Raw provider event metadata/counters beyond normalized progress remain in PRD 03 US-004.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (417 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Added src/core/provider-telemetry.ts with a best-effort provider telemetry recorder and Pi extension factory using before_provider_request and after_provider_response hooks.
Wired PiboSessionRouter to install the provider telemetry extension for routed runtimes when a telemetry store is available.

Runtime telemetry now links normalized stream progress and terminal ok/error/aborted outcomes back to active provider request rows and provider phases without storing raw provider payloads or full headers.

userStories.title: Capture bounded provider event metadata
userStories.description: As a provider engineer, I want provider event metadata, counters, and optional samples so that stream behavior is diagnosable without storing full raw event bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: For each parsed or attempted provider SSE event, record sequence, receivedAt, eventType, byteSize, parseStatus, normalizedType when produced, item id when known, and toolCallId when known
Increment provider request counters for raw events, normalized events, parse errors, and unknown event types
Malformed JSON records parseStatus invalid_json without crashing the stream parser
Unknown provider event types are counted and listed in event summaries
Per-event rows may be aggregated or sampled when volume limits require it, but counters and first/last timestamps remain queryable
Tests cover normal event, malformed JSON event, unknown event type, ignored/heartbeat-like event if applicable, and bounded event summary persistence
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in provider event metadata batch on 2026-05-16. Current Pi extension API does not expose raw OpenAI SSE bodies, so Pibo captures bounded provider parser metadata from Pi assistant stream events and store-level parse/unknown counters without editing node_modules or storing payload bodies.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'

docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run build >/tmp/pibo-test-build.log 2>&1 && node --test test/runtime-telemetry.test.mjs test/telemetry-store.test.mjs' (22 passing tests)
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (418 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Added PiboRuntimeTelemetryRecorder.recordPiEvent and wired RoutedSession/SessionRouter to capture bounded Pi assistant stream event metadata for active provider requests.

Provider event rows store event type, sequence, timestamps, byte sizes, parse status, safe structural fields, upstream response id, item/tool ids, and counters only; full provider bodies, assistant text, and full tool arguments are not stored.

Extended telemetry provider event writes with normalizedEventDelta so parser metadata can link to normalized types without double-counting normalized output events; store tests cover unknown and invalid_json counters.

userStories.title: Capture tool-call argument progress
userStories.description: As a tool engineer, I want tool-call args tracked before execution so that partial argument streams are distinguishable from tool execution failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or update telemetry tool-call rows when tool-call construction starts
Track firstDeltaAt, lastDeltaAt, argsBytes, args completion time, parseStatus, and safe top-level argument keys when parseable
Track status values for args_started, args_partial, args_complete, executing, ok, error, aborted, and timeout where observable
Link tool-call telemetry to provider item id/output index when available to avoid interleaved-call mis-correlation
Do not store full tool argument bodies by default
Tests cover empty, partial, valid-but-incomplete, invalid, complete, and never-executed tool-call argument states
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in tool telemetry batch on 2026-05-16. Captures normalized tool_call snapshots and Pi toolcall provider metadata without storing full argument bodies.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'

docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run build >/tmp/pibo-runtime-tool-build.log 2>&1 && node --test test/runtime-telemetry.test.mjs test/telemetry-store.test.mjs' (25 passing tests)
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (421 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Runtime telemetry now upserts telemetry_tool_calls rows for tool_call and Pi toolcall_start/toolcall_delta/toolcall_end metadata with toolCallId, providerRequestId, provider item id, status, first/last delta timestamps, argsBytes, argsCompletedAt, parseStatus, and safe top-level argument keys.
Tool argument telemetry stores byte counts, parse state, safe keys, ids, and timestamps only; full argument bodies are not persisted in telemetry rows.
Tests cover empty, partial JSON, invalid JSON, valid-but-incomplete, complete, and never-executed argument states.

userStories.title: Capture tool execution lifecycle telemetry
userStories.description: As an operator, I want tool execution starts and finishes linked to tool-call arguments so that failures after args completion are clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Record tool execution start time linked to toolCallId, turnId, and piboSessionId where available
Record update count or last update time for long-running tool executions where observable
Record execution end time, duration, status, and safe error category/message for ok, error, and aborted outcomes
Tool execution telemetry does not include full stdout/stderr or large payload bodies by default
Tests cover successful tool execution, failed tool execution, and started-without-finish state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in tool telemetry batch on 2026-05-16. Uses normalized tool execution events and runtime terminal events to summarize starts, progress, finishes, errors, and aborts without stdout/stderr dumps.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'

docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run build >/tmp/pibo-runtime-tool-build.log 2>&1 && node --test test/runtime-telemetry.test.mjs test/telemetry-store.test.mjs' (25 passing tests)
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (421 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Tool execution start/update/finish events now update telemetry_tool_calls with executionStartedAt, executionEndedAt, durationMs, status, updatedAt as the last-progress timestamp, and safe tool error category/message.
Runtime abort/error/timeout terminal paths now mark non-terminal tool calls with aborted/error/timeout status where observable.
Tests cover successful execution, failed execution with safe error summary, and started-without-finish execution aborted by the turn.

userStories.title: Add synthetic stuck tool-call telemetry fixture
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want a repeatable fixture for the 2026-05-16 incident pattern so that debug telemetry can be verified without live provider failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test fixture that creates a running turn with provider stream progress, a tool_args phase, a tool call with argsComplete false or equivalent partial status, and no tool execution start
Fixture includes provider raw event counts, lastRawEventAt, lastNormalizedEventAt, lastDeltaAt, and stale-capable timestamps
Fixture can be used by CLI tests without requiring network access
Tests prove the fixture produces inspectable session, turn, provider, and tool telemetry records
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in stuck tool-call fixture batch on 2026-05-16. Adds an incident-specific metadata-only fixture for downstream CLI/stale validation without network access.
validation: docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run typecheck'

docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm run build >/tmp/pibo-stuck-fixture-build.log 2>&1 && node --test test/telemetry-stuck-tool-call-fixture.test.mjs test/telemetry-store.test.mjs' (12 passing tests)
docker exec pibo-dev-ralph-observability-telemetry bash -lc 'cd /workspace && npm test' (423 passing tests)
implementationNotes: Added test/telemetry-stuck-tool-call-fixture.mjs with seedStuckToolCallTelemetryFixture(), which creates a running incident-retention turn with open provider_stream and tool_args phases, a streaming provider request, provider event metadata rows/counters, and a partial tool-call row with no execution timestamps.
Added test/telemetry-stuck-tool-call-fixture.test.mjs to prove the fixture is inspectable through session, turn, provider, provider events, tool-call, and stale store APIs.

The fixture stores ids, counters, byte counts, safe fields, timestamps, and summaries only; it does not store full provider payloads, transcripts, stdout/stderr, normalized event payload dumps, or full tool arguments.
